title: Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program: Up to US$5,000 in Funding for Early- to Mid-Career Conservationists Worldwide
summary: Wildlife conservation is one of the most urgent global priorities of our time. Around the world, biodiversity is under increasing pressure due to habitat destruction, climate change, pollution, illegal wildlife trade, invasive species, and unsustainable resource use. Addressing these complex challenges requires dedicated conservation leaders who understand local ecosystems and can develop sustainable, community-driven solutions. The Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program has been established to support exactly these leaders. Through this competitive grant initiative, Conservation Nation will provide funding and long-term professional support to conservationists working to protect threatened wildlife species, conserve biodiversity, restore habitats, and strengthen ecosystem resilience. In 2026, eight early- to mid-career conservationists will each receive grants of up to US$5,000 to implement impactful conservation projects that benefit wildlife, ecosystems, and local communities. Beyond financial support, successful applicants will become part of the Conservation Nation Network , gaining access to mentorship, professional development, capacity-building opportunities, and valuable connections within the global conservation community. About the Conservation Nation Grant Program The Conservation Nation Grant Program is designed to strengthen wildlife conservation by investing directly in conservation professionals who are creating meaningful change within their communities. Rather than focusing solely on funding projects, Conservation Nation also focuses on empowering the people behind conservation efforts. The organization believes that sustainable conservation outcomes are achieved when passionate, knowledgeable, diverse, and locally connected conservationists are equipped with the resources they need to lead. The program particularly values leadership from communities whose voices and perspectives have historically been underrepresented in conservation. By combining financial support with ongoing professional development, Conservation Nation seeks to create lasting conservation impact across terrestrial, freshwater, and marine ecosystems worldwide. Grant Value Successful applicants will receive: Grants of up to US$5,000 Membership in the Conservation Nation Network Professional development opportunities Capacity-building support Networking opportunities with conservation professionals Access to conservation resources Continued mentorship and support after receiving the grant Number of Grants Available The 2026 program will award funding to: Eight conservationists Purpose of the Grant The grant program supports initiatives that contribute to: Wildlife conservation Biodiversity protection Habitat preservation Ecosystem stewardship Community engagement Long-term biodiversity resilience Conservation leadership development The program recognizes that protecting wildlife and ecosystems requires both scientific knowledge and strong community engagement. It therefore supports conservation projects that involve local stakeholders and generate sustainable environmental outcomes. Types of Projects Supported Conservation Nation welcomes innovative, community-informed conservation initiatives from around the world. Preference will be given to projects involving: Field research Wildlife conservation Biodiversity conservation Habitat restoration Ecosystem management Community-led conservation Conservation education Ecosystem stewardship Importantly, applicants may also request funding for activities that are essential to advancing their conservation work but are often overlooked by traditional funding mechanisms. This flexible approach allows conservationists to secure support for critical components of their work that may otherwise struggle to obtain funding. Why This Grant Is Different Many conservation grants focus exclusively on project expenses. Conservation Nation takes a broader approach by investing in conservation professionals themselves. Recipients benefit from: Flexible funding Long-term professional support Access to a global conservation network Capacity-building opportunities Leadership development Ongoing mentorship Professional connections This integrated support system helps conservationists expand both their projects and their long-term careers. Conservation Nation Network Benefits Grant recipients automatically become members of the Conservation Nation Network. Network members receive: Professional development opportunities Continued learning resources Collaboration opportunities Peer networking Capacity-building support Connections with fellow conservation professionals Resources to strengthen future conservation initiatives The network is designed to ensure that grantees continue growing long after their funded projects have been completed. Funding Priorities The grant program seeks projects that contribute to: Protection of threatened wildlife species Conservation of biodiversity Habitat preservation Habitat restoration Ecosystem conservation Sustainable ecosystem management Community participation in conservation Conservation leadership Biodiversity resilience Projects that combine scientific research with meaningful community engagement are particularly encouraged. Who Should Apply? The opportunity is ideal for conservation professionals who are already working to improve biodiversity and ecosystem health. Applicants may include those working in: Wildlife conservation Ecology Biodiversity conservation Environmental science Marine conservation Freshwater conservation Habitat restoration Community conservation Natural resource management Early- and mid-career professionals seeking financial support and long-term career development are especially encouraged to apply. How to Prepare Before Applications Open Prospective applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials well before the portal opens. Preparation should include: Confirming eligibility requirements Preparing a detailed project summary Developing a realistic project budget Creating a project timeline Recording the required Role Model Video Gathering supporting documents Obtaining any necessary approvals Preparing these materials in advance can significantly improve the quality of the final application. Important Application Information Applicants should note the following: Applications must be submitted through the official grant portal. Applications sent via email will not be considered. Applicants are encouraged to review all application questions before submitting. The application portal serves as the final authority on application instructions and requirements. Requirements may be updated before the application period officially opens. Application Timeline Applicants should carefully observe the official application schedule. Applications Open: July 8, 2026, at 10:00 AM EST Application Deadline: July 15, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST The application window will remain open for one week , making early preparation especially important. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official Conservation Nation Grant Application Portal once it opens. Applicants can also: Register an account in advance. Review the application questions before submission. Download the preview of application questions. Explore previous Conservation Nation grantees for inspiration. Contact the grants team for questions. Application portal: Apply Here Final Thoughts The Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program represents an outstanding opportunity for conservation professionals committed to protecting wildlife and biodiversity. With grants of up to US$5,000 , ongoing professional development, and access to an international conservation network, the program offers much more than financial assistance. If you are leading innovative conservation work, particularly one rooted in local communities and aimed at protecting wildlife and ecosystems, this grant provides an excellent platform to expand your impact while advancing your professional journey.Be sure to prepare your materials early and submit your application before the July 15, 2026, deadline . For more information visit here 2026-grant-program-application-information Dsicover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Conservation Nation 2026 Grant Program: Up to US$5,000 in Funding for Early- to Mid-Career Conservationists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF Programme Officer (P-1) Job in Thailand 2026: Apply for the Climate Resilience for Children Position in Bangkok
summary: For professionals seeking an opportunity to build an international career while making a lasting impact on children s lives, the UNICEF Programme Officer (P-1) position in Bangkok presents an exceptional opportunity. UNICEF is recruiting a Programme Officer (P-1) on a 364-day temporary appointment within its Global Programme Division , supporting the Climate Resilience for Children initiative under the Healthy Environments for Healthy Children (HEHC) Programme . Based in Bangkok, Thailand , this role places successful candidates at the heart of UNICEF s global efforts to protect children from environmental threats, including climate change, pollution, and unhealthy built environments. Working alongside international experts, country offices, governments, and development partners, the Programme Officer will help strengthen programme delivery, financial management, knowledge sharing, and coordination across UNICEF s global network. About UNICEF UNICEF operates in more than 190 countries and territories , working tirelessly to protect children s rights, improve health and nutrition, promote education, strengthen child protection systems, and respond to humanitarian crises. The organization believes every child deserves the opportunity to survive, thrive, and reach their full potential regardless of where they are born. Employees at UNICEF become part of a diverse international workforce committed to delivering meaningful change while enjoying opportunities for professional growth, international exposure, and continuous learning. About the Position Position: Programme Officer (P-1) Contract Type: Temporary Appointment Duration: 364 days Duty Station: Bangkok, Thailand Division: Global Programme Division (GPD) Programme: Healthy Environments for Healthy Children (HEHC) Focus Area: Climate Resilience for Children The Programme Officer will provide operational, administrative, financial, and coordination support to ensure effective implementation of global programmes protecting children s health from environmental hazards. About the Healthy Environments for Healthy Children Programme The HEHC Programme focuses on improving children s health by reducing exposure to environmental risks such as: Climate change Air pollution Environmental contamination Unsafe built environments Other environmental hazards affecting child development The programme also supports the Children s Environmental Health Collaborative , bringing together global expertise to improve child health outcomes. Key Responsibilities Successful candidates will contribute to several core programme functions. 1. Global Programme Management The Programme Officer will support the implementation of international programmes by: Monitoring programme progress Tracking implementation milestones Supporting donor-funded projects Coordinating programme activities across multiple countries Ensuring compliance with UNICEF policies 2. Financial Management Financial oversight forms an important part of the role, including: Budget tracking Financial monitoring Budget transfers Monitoring expenditure Supporting donor reporting Maintaining financial records using UNICEF s VISION ERP system 3. Procurement and Contract Management Responsibilities include: Procuring technical expertise Supporting recruitment of consultants Managing contracts Processing procurement documentation Coordinating technical assistance deployment 4. Administrative Coordination The Programme Officer will also coordinate day-to-day programme operations through: Organizing meetings Managing travel arrangements Supporting administrative workflows Coordinating country office communications Maintaining programme documentation 5. Knowledge Management Knowledge-sharing responsibilities include: Managing digital collaboration platforms Supporting SharePoint systems Organizing programme information Facilitating collaboration across UNICEF offices Maintaining institutional knowledge resources Required Qualifications Applicants should possess a bachelor s degree or equivalent in one of the following areas: Social Sciences International Relations Public Administration Government Public Policy Social Policy Social Development Community Development International Business Relations Other related disciplines Required Professional Experience Applicants must have at least one year of relevant professional experience in areas such as: Programme implementation Administration Coordination Financial tracking Procurement Administrative services Programme support Required Technical Skills Strong candidates should demonstrate experience with: Budget monitoring Financial reporting Procurement procedures Contract administration Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems Data management Digital collaboration tools SharePoint or similar platforms Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in English Additional knowledge of any UN official language is considered an advantage: French Arabic Chinese Russian Spanish Desired Experience UNICEF particularly values candidates with experience in: International organizations United Nations agencies Humanitarian response Development programmes Fragile settings Executive support Programme administration Human resources processes Financial management UNICEF Core Competencies Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate the organization s CRITAS values: Care Respect Integrity Trust Accountability Sustainability Candidates should also demonstrate competencies including: Building partnerships Strategic thinking Innovation Collaboration Managing complexity Delivering results Ethical awareness Diversity and Inclusion UNICEF is committed to maintaining an inclusive workforce and strongly encourages applications from qualified candidates regardless of: Nationality Gender Race Ethnicity Religion Disability Sexual orientation Socio-economic background Reasonable accommodation is provided throughout the recruitment process for persons with disabilities. Benefits of Working with UNICEF Joining UNICEF offers numerous professional advantages, including: International career development Competitive compensation package Professional learning opportunities Global networking Diverse multicultural workplace Paid parental leave Breastfeeding breaks Disability accommodations Opportunities to contribute to global humanitarian and development initiatives Important Recruitment Information Applicants should note that: UNICEF does not charge any recruitment or application fees . Background and reference checks are mandatory. Medical clearance may be required. Visa support will be provided where applicable. Vaccination requirements may apply according to UNICEF policies. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Why You Should Apply This Programme Officer position offers an excellent entry point into an international career with one of the world s leading humanitarian organizations. Beyond administrative and programme support responsibilities, the role allows professionals to contribute directly to global efforts addressing climate resilience, environmental health, and child well-being. Working in Bangkok within UNICEF s Global Programme Division provides valuable exposure to international programme management, donor coordination, climate policy, and multi-country development initiatives. Application Deadline The specific application deadline was not stated in the information provided. Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as soon as possible through the official UNICEF Careers portal. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post UNICEF Programme Officer (P-1) Job in Thailand 2026: Apply for the Climate Resilience for Children Position in Bangkok appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Preparing for Tomorrow 2026 Grant: International Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers
summary: The Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP) has officially launched its fifth international research funding call titled “Preparing for Tomorrow – Societies and Strategies in Times of Transition.” The initiative seeks to support innovative and collaborative research aimed at helping societies understand, anticipate, and respond to future challenges in an increasingly uncertain world. As the world grapples with climate change, pandemics, geopolitical tensions, technological disruptions, economic instability, and emerging social challenges, there is a growing need for evidence-based strategies that can help communities and policymakers prepare for the future. Through this funding call, T-AP aims to strengthen international cooperation among researchers and promote interdisciplinary solutions to complex global issues. About the Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP) The Trans-Atlantic Platform for Social Sciences and Humanities is a large-scale collaboration involving research funding agencies from Europe, North America, South America, and Africa. The platform promotes international dialogue and collaborative research to address common societal challenges through social sciences and humanities research. The Preparing for Tomorrow initiative represents the fifth joint call organized by T-AP and focuses on building resilient and inclusive societies capable of navigating uncertain futures. Aim of the Grant The program will support collaborative research projects that advance understanding of how societies: Envision future scenarios. Prepare for emerging crises. Respond to uncertainty. Develop strategies for resilience and sustainable development. Inform policy and decision-making processes at local, national, and international levels. Projects are expected to generate knowledge that contributes to forward-looking and inclusive responses to societal transitions. Research Themes Applicants are invited to address one or more of the following themes: 1. Understanding Uncertainty Research may examine: Sources of uncertainty. Economic and social costs. Communication of risks. Strategies for reducing uncertainty. 2. Futures and Crises Researchers may explore: Historical perspectives on crises. Cultural interpretations of the future. Regional differences in preparing for change. Social responses to emerging threats. 3. Response Strategies and Coordination Projects may investigate: Governance systems. Coordination mechanisms. Public policy responses. Institutional preparedness. 4. Prevention and Preparation Research may focus on: Ethical and normative questions. Long-term preparedness. Prevention frameworks. Responsibility and accountability in crisis management. Key Features of the Grant The program aims to: Support international and interdisciplinary research collaboration. Promote engagement with communities and policymakers. Encourage participation and development of early-career researchers. Foster diversity within research teams. Address opportunities and risks associated with technological, political, environmental, and societal change. Eligible Disciplines The call welcomes proposals employing: Qualitative research methods. Quantitative approaches. Mixed-methods research. Historical studies. Future-oriented methodologies. Interdisciplinary approaches within Social Sciences and Humanities. Consortium Requirements Applications must involve: At least three eligible principal investigators. Participants from at least three different participating countries. Representation from both sides of the Atlantic. Compliance with national funding regulations. Applicants are encouraged to consult the national addenda provided by their funding organizations to confirm eligibility. Duration of Projects Funded projects are expected to run for: Between 24 and 36 months. Benefits of the Preparing for Tomorrow Grant Successful applicants will gain: Access to international research funding. Opportunities for transnational collaboration. Engagement with policymakers and stakeholders. Increased visibility of research outputs. Capacity-building opportunities. Support for interdisciplinary innovation. Opportunities for early-career researchers to develop internationally. Important Dates Call Launch 17 April 2026 Letter of Intent Deadline 8 July 2026 Full Application Deadline 28 October 2026 Evaluation Period March – April 2027 Funding Decisions Announced April – May 2027 Expected Start of Awards July – September 2027 How to Apply Applications are submitted through the German Research Foundation (DFG) Elan Portal by the Lead Principal Investigator on behalf of the consortium. Researchers should: Build an international consortium. Review the call scope and national addenda. Submit the Letter of Intent before 8 July 2026. Prepare and submit the full proposal by 28 October 2026. Ensure compliance with requirements established by their national funding agencies. Applicants can access the call guidelines and application documents here The Preparing for Tomorrow Grant represents a significant opportunity for social sciences and humanities researchers seeking to contribute to global preparedness and resilience. Through international collaboration and interdisciplinary approaches, the program seeks to generate new knowledge and strategies that will help societies navigate uncertainty and build a more sustainable future. Discover more global grants opportunities on OFY here The post Preparing for Tomorrow 2026 Grant: International Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: WomensNet 2026 Grants: Apply for More Than $510,000 in Funding for Women-Owned Businesses Including $10,000 Monthly Grants and $50,000 Annual Grants
summary: Women entrepreneurs often face significant challenges when trying to secure funding to launch, grow, or scale their businesses. To bridge this gap, WomensNet continues its long-standing commitment to supporting women in business through its 2026 WomensNet Grants Program , offering more than $510,000 in grant funding throughout the year . One of the biggest advantages of the WomensNet grant program is its simple application process. Instead of submitting multiple applications for different grant opportunities, applicants complete one application and are automatically considered for all grants for which they qualify. Whether you are still developing your business idea, running a startup, growing an established company, or leading a nonprofit organization, the WomensNet grants provide multiple funding opportunities throughout the year. Total Funding Available The WomensNet 2026 Grants Program provides more than US$510,000 in funding through: Monthly Amber Grants Monthly Startup Grants Monthly Business Category Grants Annual Amber Grants Successful applicants may even become eligible for additional year-end funding worth US$50,000 . Why Apply? Applicants only need to complete one application to be considered for multiple grant opportunities. Benefits include: More than US$510,000 available annually. One application covers all eligible grants. Monthly funding opportunities. Additional chances to receive year-end grants. Support for startups and established businesses. Nonprofit organizations are also eligible. Wide variety of industry-specific grants. 1. Monthly Amber Grant The flagship WomensNet funding opportunity is the Monthly Amber Grant . Each month, one outstanding woman-owned business receives: US$10,000 grant Eligible businesses include: Small businesses Established businesses Growing businesses Women-led nonprofit organizations Businesses from all industries Applications are carefully reviewed each month before one winner is selected. Additional Benefit Every Monthly Amber Grant recipient automatically advances to compete for the: US$50,000 Annual Amber Grant This provides successful applicants with an opportunity to secure even larger funding at the end of the year. 2. Monthly Startup Grant Recognizing that early-stage businesses often struggle the most to access funding, WomensNet also offers the Monthly Startup Grant . Each month: One startup receives US$10,000 . WomensNet defines a startup as: A business still in the idea stage, or A business with less than US$10,000 in sales . Although startups are already eligible for the Monthly Amber Grant, WomensNet created this additional funding opportunity specifically to support entrepreneurs at the earliest stages of business development. Additional Benefit Monthly Startup Grant winners also become eligible for the: US$50,000 Annual Startup Grant 3. Business Category Grants Women entrepreneurs operating within specific industries may qualify for the Business Category Grants . Each category awards: US$10,000 One of the most attractive features of this program is that once an applicant submits an Amber Grant application, it remains active for consideration under the relevant business category for 12 months . There is no need to submit another application . 2026 Business Category Grant Schedule January Skilled Trades US$10,000 February Health Fitness US$10,000 March Food Beverage US$10,000 April Sustainability US$10,000 May Mental Emotional Support US$10,000 June Business Support Services US$10,000 July Animal Services US$10,000 August Hair Care Skincare US$10,000 September Education Child Care US$10,000 October Creative Arts US$10,000 November STEM US$10,000 December Fashion Interior Design US$10,000 Additional Benefit Each Business Category Grant winner automatically becomes eligible for the: US$50,000 Annual Business Category Grant 4. Annual Amber Grants At the end of each year, WomensNet awards three separate US$50,000 grants . These grants recognize the year s outstanding winners from: Monthly Amber Grants Monthly Startup Grants Business Category Grants This means that monthly winners have the opportunity to receive substantial additional funding without submitting another application. One Application for Multiple Grants One of the most valuable aspects of the WomensNet grant system is its streamlined application process. With a single application , applicants are automatically considered for: Monthly Amber Grant Monthly Startup Grant (if eligible) Monthly Business Category Grant (if eligible) Annual US$50,000 Grant opportunities This greatly increases the chances of receiving funding. Who Should Apply? The program is ideal for: Women launching new businesses. Entrepreneurs developing business ideas. Existing women-owned businesses seeking growth capital. Women-owned nonprofit organizations. Businesses operating in any industry. Entrepreneurs working within one of the featured annual business categories. Funding Summary Grant Funding Monthly Amber Grant US$10,000 Monthly Startup Grant US$10,000 Monthly Business Category Grant US$10,000 Annual Amber Grant US$50,000 (3 winners annually) Deadline There is no single annual deadline . Applications are accepted throughout the year, and applicants are considered according to the monthly grant cycle. Business Category Grant applications remain active for 12 months after submission. How to Apply Applicants only need to submit one application through the official WomensNet application portal to be considered for all eligible grants. Prospective applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize eligibility for monthly and annual funding opportunities. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Grants opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post WomensNet 2026 Grants: Apply for More Than $510,000 in Funding for Women-Owned Businesses Including $10,000 Monthly Grants and $50,000 Annual Grants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OPCW Scholarships 2026 for the Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) in The Hague, Netherlands
summary: Professionals working in arms control, non-proliferation, international security, diplomacy, and chemical weapons policy now have an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their expertise through one of the world’s most prestigious disarmament training programmes. Applications are now open for the 2026 Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD), jointly organized by the Asser Institute and the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) in The Hague, Netherlands. As part of this initiative, the OPCW is offering tuition fee waiver scholarships funded by the European Union for selected participants working in civil society, NGOs, think tanks, and academia. The intensive five-day programme will take place from 26 October to 30 October 2026 and provides participants with advanced practical and policy-oriented training on the rapidly evolving global challenges surrounding weapons of mass destruction, arms control, export regulations, emerging technologies, and international law. Programme Overview The international arms control landscape is currently facing unprecedented pressure due to geopolitical fragmentation, technological disruption, and the weakening of global non-proliferation frameworks. Concerns over nuclear weapons have returned to global political discourse, chemical weapons continue to pose major threats in active conflicts, and emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and biotechnology are reshaping traditional security environments faster than existing legal systems can adapt. Against this backdrop, the Asser–OPCW Training Programme was designed to equip professionals with the legal, diplomatic, technical, and strategic knowledge required to navigate today’s complex disarmament and non-proliferation challenges. Participants will engage directly with leading international experts, practitioners, policymakers, and researchers through interactive lectures, discussions, case studies, and field visits. Scholarship Benefits Selected scholarship recipients will receive: Full tuition fee waiver worth €1,895 Access to expert-led sessions and workshops Networking opportunities with international security professionals Exposure to high-level practitioners in arms control and disarmament Participation in exclusive institutional field visits Advanced practical understanding of modern WMD challenges Professional development in international law, export controls, and security governance The scholarships are funded by the European Union and specifically support professionals from civil society organizations and academic sectors. Female applicants and candidates from lower-income countries are strongly encouraged to apply. Programme Dates and Location Dates: 26 October – 30 October 2026 Duration: Five days Venue: Asser Institute and OPCW Location: The Hague, Netherlands Address: R.J. Schimmelpennincklaan 20-22 2517 JN The Hague Netherlands Key Topics Covered During the Programme Participants will receive comprehensive training on some of the world’s most urgent disarmament and security challenges. 1. Nuclear Weapons and Strategic Stability Sessions will examine: Expanding nuclear stockpiles Declining arms control agreements Challenges facing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) Deadlocked NPT Review Conferences Future pathways for nuclear disarmament 2. Chemical Weapons and Accountability Participants will explore: Use of chemical weapons in modern conflicts International accountability mechanisms Verification and compliance systems Challenges of misinformation and disinformation Enforcement of chemical weapons conventions 3. Biological Weapons and Synthetic Biology Key discussions include: Risks posed by synthetic biology Advances in pathogen engineering Weaknesses in the Biological Weapons Convention Biosecurity governance challenges Future biological weapons threats 4. Export Controls and Emerging Technologies The programme also addresses rapidly developing technologies, including: Artificial intelligence-enabled systems Autonomous weapons Hypersonic missile technology Export control regulations Legal and ethical gaps in emerging military technologies Exclusive Field Visits One of the most valuable components of the programme is direct institutional exposure through guided visits to internationally significant facilities. Participants will visit: OPCW Centre for Chemistry and Technology (ChemTech) This advanced facility supports scientific research, verification training, and international cooperation related to chemical weapons prohibition. OPCW Headquarters Participants will gain firsthand insight into the operations of one of the world’s leading international organizations dedicated to eliminating chemical weapons globally. Confirmed International Experts and Speakers The programme brings together highly respected scholars and practitioners from globally recognized institutions. Confirmed speakers include: Dr León Castellanos-Jankiewicz – Asser Institute Simon Cleobury – Geneva Centre for Security Policy Dr Sico van der Meer – Clingendael Institute Dr Oliver Meier – European Leadership Network Ionut Suseanu – International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Dr Ana Sánchez-Cobaleda – University of Barcelona Dr Sibylle Bauer – Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) Dr James Revill – United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR) Dr Miranda Smith – SIPRI Dr Edward Howell – University of Oxford Dr Abdolrasool Farzam Divsallar – UNIDIR Who Should Apply? This programme is ideal for professionals and students seeking specialized expertise in international security and disarmament policy. Suitable applicants include: Government officials Diplomats NGO professionals Think tank researchers Academic scholars International organization staff Compliance officers Legal advisers Graduate students transitioning into the security field Applicants with backgrounds in international relations, law, diplomacy, political science, security studies, public policy, arms control, and strategic affairs are especially encouraged to apply. Why This Programme Matters The global security environment is evolving rapidly. Traditional arms control systems are increasingly challenged by geopolitical tensions, technological innovation, and the growing complexity of modern warfare. This programme provides participants with: Practical policy knowledge Exposure to real-world disarmament challenges International networking opportunities Institutional access to global security actors Specialized expertise highly valued in diplomacy and international organizations For professionals seeking careers in international security, arms control, diplomacy, compliance, or multilateral policy-making, this programme represents a significant career-development opportunity. Tuition Fee Standard Programme Fee: €1,895 Scholarship Support: Tuition fee waivers available through OPCW scholarships Application Deadline 1 August 2026 Learn More Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the OPCW Scholarships 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: Explore 12 Free FAO eLearning Academy Courses on Sustainability, Food Security, Nutrition, Biodiversity, Forestry, and Agriculture in 2026
summary: The global transition toward more sustainable food systems requires skilled professionals, informed policymakers, empowered communities, and knowledgeable young leaders. To support this transformation, the FAO eLearning Academy has launched an impressive collection of free online sustainability courses covering critical topics ranging from biodiversity and climate-smart agriculture to food loss reduction, nutrition, gender equality, agroforestry, and responsible investment. These self-paced online courses have been designed by experts from the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations to strengthen knowledge, develop practical skills, and support sustainable development worldwide. Whether you are a student, researcher, government official, development practitioner, nutritionist, farmer, environmental expert, or simply passionate about sustainable food systems, these learning opportunities provide valuable knowledge completely free of charge. In addition to the online courses, FAO is also inviting young people to participate in its Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact , providing an exciting opportunity to showcase innovative ideas and contribute to sustainable development. Why Enroll in FAO eLearning Academy Courses? The FAO eLearning Academy has become one of the world s leading platforms for capacity building in agriculture, food security, nutrition, climate resilience, and rural development. Participants benefit from: Free access to world-class learning resources. Self-paced online study. Courses developed by international experts. Practical knowledge applicable to real-world challenges. Global perspectives on sustainable development. Learning materials aligned with international standards. Opportunities to strengthen professional skills. Enhanced career prospects in agriculture, nutrition, environmental management, and international development. Complete List of Available FAO Sustainability Courses 1. Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact The Global Youth Contest encourages young people to transform the knowledge gained through FAO learning into practical solutions that address global food security, sustainability, and environmental challenges. Participants have the opportunity to showcase innovative ideas that can create positive impact in their communities and beyond. Deadline: 31 July 2026 2. Multidimensional Solutions to Reduce Food Loss and Waste in Fish Value Chains Food loss and waste remain major global challenges affecting food security and economic development. This course explores: Causes of food loss in fisheries Sustainable fish value chains Waste reduction strategies Policy interventions Best practices across the fisheries sector 3. Costing and Monetizing Benefits for Nutrition This course introduces participants to economic approaches used to evaluate nutrition investments. Topics include: Cost-benefit analysis Economic valuation Nutrition investment planning Measuring returns on nutrition interventions Evidence-based policy development 4. Biodiversity and Agrifood Systems – Introduction Biodiversity is essential for resilient food systems. Participants learn about: Biodiversity conservation Ecosystem services Sustainable agriculture Food system resilience Environmental sustainability 5. Introduction to Documenting Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture Plant genetic resources are fundamental to global food security. This course explains: Plant genetic diversity Documentation methods Conservation systems Germplasm management Agricultural biodiversity 6. Voluntary Guidelines on Gender Equality and Women s and Girls Empowerment in the Context of Food Security and Nutrition Gender equality plays a central role in achieving sustainable development. The course covers: Women s empowerment Food security Gender-responsive policies Inclusive agriculture Nutrition governance International voluntary guidelines 7. Agrifood Systems Solutions for Halting Deforestation Agricultural expansion remains one of the leading causes of deforestation. Participants explore: Sustainable land management Deforestation prevention Responsible supply chains Environmental governance Climate change mitigation 8. Trade and Nutrition: Policy Coherence for Healthy Diets Trade policies directly influence food availability and nutrition outcomes. Topics include: Nutrition-sensitive trade policies Healthy diets Policy coherence Food systems governance International trade frameworks 9. Farmers Markets: An Introduction Farmers markets strengthen local food systems and rural economies. This introductory course discusses: Local food markets Market organization Value chain development Farmer participation Consumer engagement Sustainable local economies 10. Empower Women to Engage in Responsible Investment in Agriculture and Food Systems Women s participation in agricultural investment is essential for inclusive growth. Participants will learn about: Responsible agricultural investment Gender-inclusive finance Women s economic empowerment Sustainable agribusiness Food systems development 11. Agroforestry for Sustainable Agrifood Transformation – Introduction Agroforestry integrates trees into agricultural landscapes to improve productivity and sustainability. The course examines: Agroforestry systems Climate resilience Soil conservation Biodiversity Sustainable farming Carbon sequestration 12. Enhancing Policy Coherence Through Public Expenditure Analysis for Forests Effective forest governance depends on sound public investment. Topics include: Forest financing Public expenditure analysis Policy coherence Sustainable forest management Governance tools Budget planning Who Should Take These Courses? These learning opportunities are highly relevant for: University students Graduate students Researchers Nutritionists Agricultural extension workers Forestry professionals Environmental scientists Government officials Policymakers NGO professionals International development practitioners Food systems experts Climate professionals Sustainable development advocates Skills You Can Develop Completing these courses can help participants strengthen knowledge in: Sustainable agriculture Food security Nutrition economics Biodiversity conservation Agroforestry Forestry governance Responsible investment Gender equality Climate resilience Food systems transformation Agricultural policy Public expenditure analysis Trade policy Fisheries management Why These Courses Matter The world is facing interconnected challenges including climate change, biodiversity loss, food insecurity, malnutrition, deforestation, and unsustainable agricultural practices. Building knowledge and professional capacity is essential for addressing these issues. The FAO eLearning Academy equips learners with internationally recognized knowledge that supports the achievement of multiple Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including Zero Hunger, Climate Action, Life on Land, Gender Equality, and Responsible Consumption and Production. Whether your goal is career advancement, academic development, policy implementation, or community impact, these free courses offer valuable learning opportunities from one of the world s leading organizations in agriculture and food security. Deadline Most of the online courses are available on a rolling basis. However, the Global Youth Contest: From Learning to Impact has an application deadline of 31 July 2026 . title: Embassy of Denmark in Ghana Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Communication and Cultural Officer Position in Accra
summary: The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana has announced an exciting career opportunity for talented communication professionals seeking to contribute to international relations, cultural diplomacy, and public engagement. Applications are now open for the position of Communication and Cultural Officer , a role designed for highly motivated individuals with strong communication skills, creativity, and a passion for strengthening international partnerships. Based in Accra, Ghana, the successful candidate will play a central role in enhancing Denmark’s visibility, public diplomacy efforts, and cultural engagement across Ghana and several side-accredited countries covered by the Embassy. If you have experience in communications, event management, journalism, public relations, cultural programming, or international affairs, this position offers an excellent opportunity to work within a respected diplomatic mission while contributing to impactful cultural and communication initiatives. About the Embassy of Denmark in Ghana The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana is part of the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs and serves as Denmark’s official diplomatic representation in Ghana. In addition to Ghana, the Embassy also covers several side-accredited countries, including: Guinea Ivory Coast Liberia Sierra Leone São Tomé and Príncipe Togo The Embassy works to strengthen diplomatic relations, promote Danish interests, support development cooperation, and foster cultural exchange between Denmark and countries within its area of responsibility. Position Overview Job Title Communication and Cultural Officer Employment Type Full-time contract (40 hours per week), or Part-time contract under local terms and conditions Location 67 Dr. Isert Street, North Ridge, Accra, Ghana Start Date As soon as possible Application Deadline Wednesday, 8 July 2026 Role Summary The Communication and Cultural Officer will be responsible for ensuring effective and continuous communication on behalf of the Embassy while strengthening Denmark’s public image and cultural presence in Ghana and neighboring countries under the Embassy’s accreditation. The role combines strategic communication, public diplomacy, stakeholder engagement, event coordination, and cultural project management. The selected candidate will work closely with Embassy staff, media professionals, cultural stakeholders, and international partners to showcase Danish priorities, initiatives, and partnerships. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will undertake a variety of communication and cultural diplomacy functions, including: 1. Lead Cultural Programming and Events The officer will plan, coordinate, and execute a range of cultural and diplomatic activities, including: High-level diplomatic events Official visits Seminars and conferences Networking engagements Cultural exhibitions Public diplomacy initiatives These activities will help strengthen Denmark’s profile and visibility in Ghana and other countries covered by the Embassy. 2. Develop Communication Content The role involves creating compelling communication materials that effectively communicate Embassy priorities and activities. Examples include: Articles Speeches Press releases Presentations Visual communication materials Public information content The selected candidate must be capable of adapting messages to different audiences and communication channels. 3. Manage Digital Communication Platforms The Communication and Cultural Officer will oversee the Embassy’s communication channels and ensure a consistent online presence. Responsibilities include: Managing social media accounts Updating website content Supporting digital campaigns Monitoring online engagement Producing content for digital audiences 4. Build Strategic Media Relationships A key aspect of the role is developing and maintaining professional relationships with: Journalists Media organizations Communication professionals Cultural attachés International organizations Diplomatic missions Strong stakeholder engagement will support the Embassy’s communication objectives and public diplomacy efforts. 5. Coordinate Cultural Projects The successful candidate will also oversee cultural initiatives related to: Music Literature Performing arts Exhibitions Creative industries Cultural exchange programs These projects will help deepen cultural connections and strengthen bilateral relationships. Required Qualifications Applicants must possess a strong academic background and relevant professional experience. Minimum requirements include: A Bachelor s degree or equivalent qualification in a relevant discipline such as: Communications Journalism Event Management Public Relations Media Studies Related fields Demonstrated experience managing events and communication campaigns. Excellent command of written and spoken English. Preferred Skills and Competencies The Embassy is looking for a candidate who can demonstrate: Strong Communication Skills Applicants should possess excellent verbal and written communication abilities and be able to communicate effectively with diverse audiences. Storytelling Ability The successful candidate should be capable of creating engaging narratives that highlight diplomatic, cultural, and development initiatives. Collaboration and Teamwork The position requires working closely with colleagues, stakeholders, media representatives, and external partners. Creativity and Innovation Creative thinking and the ability to develop fresh communication and cultural engagement ideas are highly valued. Cultural Awareness Candidates should demonstrate sensitivity to cultural diversity and an appreciation for international cooperation. Professional Growth Mindset The Embassy seeks individuals who are eager to learn, share knowledge, and continuously improve their professional skills. Interest in International Affairs A strong interest in diplomacy, international relations, development cooperation, and global affairs will be an advantage. Additional Advantages While not mandatory, the following qualifications will strengthen an application: Proficiency in French Previous experience working at: Embassies International organizations Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Multicultural institutions Employment Benefits The Embassy offers an attractive employment package under local terms and conditions. Benefits include: Flexible Employment Options Applicants may be considered for: Full-time employment Part-time employment Annual Leave Employees receive: Five weeks of paid annual leave Flexible Work Arrangements The Embassy offers a flexible work scheme to support work-life balance. Health Insurance Coverage The successful candidate and eligible dependent family members will receive health insurance that includes coverage for medical expenses. Competitive Salary Salary will be determined according to: Qualifications Relevant professional experience Demonstrated work performance Embassy salary scale guidelines Probation Period The employment contract includes a mutual probation period of three months. Security Clearance Requirement Employment is subject to security clearance by Danish authorities. Applicants should be prepared to provide: A valid No Criminal Record certificate Failure to obtain the required security clearance may affect employment eligibility. How to Apply Interested candidates must submit their applications in English. Application packages should include: Brief cover letter One-page Curriculum Vitae (CV) Educational qualification documents Relevant recommendations Contact details of one or two references Submission Instructions Applications should be sent by email with the subject line: Communication and Cultural Officer All documents must be submitted in PDF format, either: As separate PDF attachments, or Combined into a single PDF document Application Deadline 8 July 2026 Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered. Recruitment Process Following the application review process: Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews. Interviews are expected to take place within two weeks after the application deadline. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. Equal Opportunity Commitment The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana is committed to equal employment opportunities and welcomes applications from all qualified individuals regardless of: Race Sex Religion Age Disability status The Embassy values diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from candidates of varied backgrounds. Data Protection and Privacy The Embassy processes job applications in accordance with the European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). By submitting an application, candidates consent to the processing of their personal data for recruitment purposes. Applicants are encouraged to review the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs privacy notice before applying to understand: Data processing procedures Applicant rights Consent withdrawal options Complaint procedures regarding personal data handling Why This Opportunity Stands Out The Communication and Cultural Officer position offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of diplomacy, communications, culture, and international engagement. Successful candidates will contribute directly to strengthening Denmark’s visibility and partnerships across West Africa while gaining valuable experience within an international diplomatic environment. Professionals with a passion for storytelling, cultural exchange, public diplomacy, and strategic communication are strongly encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline of 8 July 2026 . Please send your application by email to accamb.recruitment@um.dk , with Communication and Cultural Officer in the subject line. Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . title: University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026–2027 in the United States
summary: The University of Maryland School of Pharmacy is inviting applications for its highly regarded Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship within the Department of Pharmacy Practice, Sciences, and Health Outcomes Research (P-SHOR). This fellowship is hosted through the renowned PATIENTS Program at the University of Maryland, Baltimore (UMB), offering scholars a unique opportunity to gain advanced multidisciplinary training in community-engaged research while working alongside leading researchers, community partners, healthcare stakeholders, and funding agencies. This postdoctoral fellowship is designed for individuals who have completed a PhD or equivalent doctoral degree and are interested in advancing their careers in academia, government, pharmaceutical industries, healthcare research, and consulting. Fellows will receive extensive mentorship, hands-on research experience, grant-writing support, and opportunities to contribute to impactful projects funded by major organizations such as the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ), the Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the National Institutes of Health (NIH), the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI), pharmaceutical companies, and private foundations. Located in Baltimore, Maryland, this fellowship provides a dynamic environment where fellows can develop expertise in community-engaged research while contributing to meaningful initiatives that improve healthcare outcomes and strengthen community partnerships. About the Community-Engaged Research Fellowship The Community-Engaged Research (CEnR) Postdoctoral Fellowship is part of the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy’s commitment to advancing patient-centered and community-focused healthcare research. The fellowship offers structured and mentored training experiences that prepare fellows for leadership positions across multiple sectors. Participants will work closely with experienced investigators and community organizations while gaining practical exposure to all phases of the research process. The fellowship emphasizes: Community-engaged research methodologies Patient-centered outcomes research Qualitative research approaches Grant proposal development Scientific manuscript preparation Research dissemination Collaborative engagement with community stakeholders Academic and professional development Fellows will become part of a vibrant research ecosystem consisting of more than 20 active projects supported by the PATIENTS Program. Fellowship Location The fellowship will be based at: University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Baltimore, Maryland, United States The institution is widely recognized for excellence in pharmacy education, healthcare innovation, scientific research, and community engagement. Fellowship Start Date The fellowship offers flexibility for selected candidates. Start Date: Flexible and negotiable, beginning anytime from Fall 2026 through Summer 2027. This flexibility allows successful applicants to coordinate their transition into the fellowship based on their academic, professional, or personal schedules. Fellowship Salary Selected fellows will receive a competitive annual salary ranging from: $63,480 – $77,076 Compensation is determined based on: NIH stipend guidelines Educational qualifications Relevant experience Professional background Fellowship Benefits Participants will gain access to numerous professional and academic benefits, including: 1. Advanced Research Training Fellows receive intensive training in community-engaged and patient-centered research methodologies. 2. Mentorship from Leading Researchers Participants will work under the guidance of distinguished investigators, including: C. Daniel Mullins (Primary Investigator) Claudia Baquet Jasmine K. Cooper Hillary Edwards Michelle Medeiros 3. Grant Writing Experience The fellowship includes practical training in developing competitive grant applications for major funding agencies and foundations. 4. Scientific Publishing Opportunities Fellows will gain valuable experience in manuscript preparation, publication strategies, and scholarly dissemination. 5. Extensive Community Engagement Participants will collaborate directly with community organizations, patients, healthcare professionals, and research partners. 6. Optional Coursework Fellows may enroll in coursework related to: Community-Engaged Research (CEnR) Patient-Centered Outcomes Research (PCOR) 7. Career Development The fellowship prepares participants for future careers in: Universities and academic institutions Government agencies Pharmaceutical companies Healthcare organizations Research institutions Consulting firms 8. Competitive Employee Benefits Eligible fellows may access University of Maryland Baltimore postdoctoral benefits and support programs. Research Areas and Responsibilities Selected fellows will participate in a broad range of activities that strengthen their research and professional skills. Key responsibilities include: Conducting qualitative research studies Leading focus groups and key informant interviews Analyzing qualitative data Engaging community stakeholders Collaborating with multidisciplinary research teams Preparing manuscripts for publication Supporting grant proposal development Contributing to ongoing community-engaged research initiatives Presenting research findings The fellowship offers extensive exposure to real-world healthcare challenges and patient-centered solutions. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following requirements: Required Qualifications Possess a PhD or equivalent doctoral degree Demonstrate strong qualitative research skills Have experience conducting focus groups Have experience conducting key informant interviews Demonstrate qualitative data analysis expertise Possess excellent communication skills Have strong organizational abilities Demonstrate excellent academic writing skills Possess effective time-management skills Be capable of working empathetically within community settings Preferred Qualifications Applicants with experience in the following areas are particularly encouraged to apply: Community-Engaged Research (CEnR) Community-Based Participatory Research (CBPR) Patient-centered research initiatives Community partnership development Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following materials: Letter of Intent Curriculum Vitae (CV) Writing Sample Three Letters of Recommendation Incomplete applications may not receive full consideration. Application Process Interested applicants should submit their application through the University of Maryland s online recruitment system using: Taleo Requisition Number: 260000MQ Applications are currently being reviewed. Application Deadline No fixed deadline has been announced. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and will continue until suitable candidates are selected. Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of consideration. About the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Founded in 1841, the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy is the fourth-oldest school of pharmacy in the United States. The institution is internationally recognized for its contributions to: Pharmacy education Scientific discovery Patient care Community engagement Healthcare innovation The School s mission is centered on improving health outcomes through research excellence, education, and meaningful community partnerships. Why This Fellowship Stands Out This fellowship provides a rare opportunity to combine advanced qualitative research training with meaningful community engagement. Fellows benefit from direct mentorship, exposure to large-scale funded projects, publication opportunities, grant-writing experience, and collaborative research that addresses real healthcare needs. For doctoral graduates seeking to build impactful careers in healthcare research, academia, policy, or industry, the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship represents an exceptional professional development opportunity. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026–2027 in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Institute for Gender Equality Gender Equality Forum 2026 Youth Participation Call: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young People Across the European Union
summary: Young people across Europe now have a unique opportunity to contribute directly to important discussions shaping the future of gender equality, economic growth, and competitiveness within the European Union. The European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE) has officially launched a call for youth participation in the Gender Equality Forum 2026, inviting 35 young individuals to attend the prestigious event in Brussels, Belgium, on 17 November 2026. This exciting initiative is designed to ensure that youth perspectives are fully integrated into conversations about gender equality and its role in strengthening Europe’s economic and social future. Selected participants will have the opportunity to engage with policymakers, experts, employers, civil society representatives, and fellow young leaders from across Europe. Importantly, EIGE will provide travel support for up to 30 selected participants through reimbursement of travel expenses, accommodation costs, and daily allowances, making the opportunity accessible to a diverse group of young people. About the European Institute for Gender Equality The European Institute for Gender Equality serves as the European Union’s knowledge centre for gender equality. As the EU agency dedicated to promoting gender equality, EIGE develops evidence-based research, tools, and resources that support policymakers in creating inclusive and transformative policies across all sectors of society. Through initiatives such as the Gender Equality Forum, EIGE brings together stakeholders from different backgrounds to address challenges and opportunities related to equality, inclusion, and sustainable development across Europe. What is the Gender Equality Forum 2026? The Gender Equality Forum is one of Europe’s leading platforms for discussing pressing gender equality issues affecting the European Union. The 2026 edition arrives at a crucial moment when several major European priorities are intersecting, including: Care policies and support systems Ongoing discussions regarding the EU s multiannual budget Innovation and competitiveness strategies Labour market inclusion Economic resilience and growth The Forum seeks to ensure that gender equality remains central to Europe’s economic and social development agenda. Recognizing that young people are key stakeholders in Europe’s future, EIGE is placing a strong emphasis on youth participation and is actively seeking young voices to contribute ideas, experiences, and solutions. Key Themes of the Gender Equality Forum 2026 Discussions throughout the Forum will focus on three major thematic areas: 1. Care at the Core for Resilient Economies Participants will explore how care systems contribute to stronger economies and societies, including discussions around: Care infrastructure Work-life balance Family support systems Social protection policies Economic resilience 2. Skills for Innovation: Addressing Gender Gaps in Opportunities and Workplaces This theme will examine ways to reduce gender disparities in: Education Skills development Employment opportunities Workplace advancement Innovation ecosystems Participants will discuss how equal access to opportunities can foster innovation and sustainable growth. 3. The Cost of Gender Gaps and Unlocking Gender Equality as an Economic Accelerator This strand will focus on: Economic consequences of gender inequality Workforce participation gaps Pay disparities Productivity impacts Strategies for promoting inclusive economic growth Discussions will highlight how advancing gender equality benefits not only individuals but entire economies. What to Expect at the Forum The Gender Equality Forum 2026 will offer a diverse and engaging programme designed to encourage meaningful participation and collaboration. Activities will include: High-level debates Interactive workshops Expert panel discussions Inspirational keynote talks Networking opportunities Collaborative problem-solving sessions Participants will engage with: Representatives of European Union institutions Civil society organizations Gender equality experts Employers and business leaders Researchers Artists and cultural contributors Fellow young changemakers The event will be conducted entirely in English. To enhance accessibility, selected sessions will include International Sign Language interpretation both onsite and online. Benefits of Participating in the Gender Equality Forum 2026 Young participants selected for the Forum will enjoy a range of professional, personal, and networking benefits. Financial Support For up to 30 selected participants, EIGE will provide reimbursement for: Travel expenses Accommodation costs Daily allowances This support helps remove financial barriers and promotes diverse participation from across the European Union. Direct Access to High-Level Decision Makers Participants will have opportunities to interact with: EU policymakers Senior institutional representatives Gender equality specialists Industry leaders Progressive employers This exposure can provide valuable insights into policymaking and advocacy. Platform to Share Your Voice Young attendees will be encouraged to actively contribute their ideas and perspectives throughout the event. Participants may: Ask questions during discussions Share experiences and viewpoints Contribute recommendations Engage in collaborative solutions-building Professional Networking Opportunities The Forum creates a valuable environment for building relationships with: Peers from across Europe Experts in gender equality Employers committed to inclusion Civil society leaders These connections can support future career and advocacy opportunities. Active Participation in Forum Activities Selected youth participants may also have opportunities to: Appear in the Forum opening video Be featured on EIGE’s website Participate in question-and-answer sessions Join thematic workshops Contribute to discussions with EIGE leadership Help document and promote the event through EIGE’s social media platforms Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Be between 18 and 30 years old at the time of application Reside in one of the 27 European Union Member States Non-EU nationals residing within the EU are also eligible Have gained work experience through employment, traineeship, internship, or similar activities Demonstrate commitment to gender equality as a core European value Be motivated to actively participate in Forum discussions and activities Be comfortable communicating in English Be available to attend the event in person in Brussels, Belgium, on 17 November 2026 Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion The European Institute for Gender Equality strongly encourages applications from diverse backgrounds. Applications are welcomed regardless of: Gender Gender identity Sexual orientation Racial background Ethnic origin Social background Disability status Religion or belief Colour This commitment reflects EIGE’s mission of promoting equality and inclusion throughout Europe. Application Process Interested candidates must complete and submit the official online application form provided by the European Institute for Gender Equality. Applicants should ensure that they clearly demonstrate: Their interest in gender equality Relevant work or internship experience Motivation for participation Ability to contribute actively to Forum discussions Application Deadline Deadline: 7 July 2026 (End of Business Day) Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible to avoid last-minute issues. Why This Opportunity Matters The Gender Equality Forum 2026 offers much more than attendance at a conference. It provides young people with a genuine opportunity to influence discussions surrounding the future of gender equality and economic development within the European Union. By bringing together youth voices, policymakers, experts, employers, and civil society actors, the Forum creates a collaborative environment where innovative ideas can shape future policies and actions. For young Europeans passionate about equality, inclusion, economic progress, and social change, this is a rare opportunity to engage directly with decision-makers while building valuable networks and gaining meaningful experience at the European level. If you are ready to contribute your voice to one of Europe’s most important conversations on gender equality, now is the time to apply. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Conference Opportunities on OFY here . title: Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity)
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming nearly every aspect of modern life—from education and healthcare to governance, journalism, business, and communication. As AI systems become increasingly integrated into society, there is an urgent need for experts who can critically examine how these technologies influence humanity, ethics, public policy, culture, and everyday life. To help address these challenges, the Center for Humane Technology (CHT) has launched its inaugural Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship , a prestigious six-month remote fellowship designed to support interdisciplinary professionals who are committed to advancing thoughtful conversations and impactful research on AI and society. The fellowship offers a $30,000 stipend , extensive mentorship, editorial support, media training, and an opportunity to become part of a growing community of experts shaping the future of responsible AI. If you are a mid-career researcher, policy professional, journalist, advocate, technologist, or thought leader interested in AI s societal impacts, this fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity to amplify your work while contributing to one of the world s most important conversations. Application Deadline: July 12, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST About the Center for Humane Technology (CHT) The Center for Humane Technology (CHT) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to ensuring that technology serves humanity s best interests. Rather than focusing solely on technological advancement, CHT works to understand how emerging technologies influence individuals, communities, institutions, and society as a whole. The organization aims to: Clarify the societal impacts of rapidly evolving technologies. Connect everyday technological harms to larger systemic issues. Promote responsible technology development. Encourage informed public conversations. Support practical solutions that align innovation with human values. Recognizing that these complex challenges require diverse perspectives, CHT established the Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship to empower experts from multiple disciplines to contribute meaningful research, public engagement, and innovative ideas. Fellowship Overview The Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship is a six-month remote program that supports professionals exploring critical questions about AI s impact on society. Fellowship Details Fellowship Type: Remote Duration: September 14, 2026 – March 18, 2027 Commitment: 15 hours per week Required overlap: At least 10 hours during Eastern Standard Time (EST) Fellowship Cohort: 3 Fellows Stipend: $30,000 Payment Structure: Paid upon completion of three program milestones Fellowship Focus Areas Applicants may apply to one or two of the following research areas. 1. AI and Cognition This area examines how AI affects: Human thinking Learning Decision-making Critical thinking Intellectual independence Creativity Cognitive development in adults and children Diversity of thought Human-machine collaboration Researchers are encouraged to investigate whether AI merely enhances human capabilities or fundamentally changes the way people think. 2. AI and Relationships This theme explores how artificial intelligence influences: Human relationships Social interaction Communication Emotional wellbeing Community building Human connection 3. AI and Surveillance Applicants may investigate topics including: Privacy Data collection Digital monitoring Government surveillance Corporate surveillance Civil liberties Human rights 4. AI and Spirituality Potential research areas include: Human meaning Identity Faith Values Purpose Spiritual wellbeing The philosophical implications of AI 5. Inside the Machine: AI from a Technologist s Perspective Ideal for professionals with technical backgrounds interested in explaining: AI system development Model design Engineering decisions Technical trade-offs Industry practices AI infrastructure Responsible AI development Fellowship Expectations Selected fellows will actively contribute to CHT s public engagement and research initiatives. Each fellow will be expected to: Complete one flagship research output such as: Research paper Long-form essay Public report Produce between one and three opinion articles (op-eds) for major publications or CHT s Substack. Participate in a podcast episode on Your Undivided Attention or another CHT platform where appropriate. Contribute to CHT s media and communications ecosystem. Attend: Three mandatory cohort meetings. Mandatory bi-weekly meetings with the Senior Director of Strategy and Impact. Fellowship Benefits Participants receive far more than financial support. Benefits include: Financial Support $30,000 stipend Milestone-based payments Professional Mentorship Bi-weekly guidance from CHT s Senior Director of Strategy and Impact Research support from the CHT Research Team Communications Support Editorial assistance Graphic design support Communications support Media training Public relations assistance Media placement opportunities Professional Visibility Fellows will also receive: A CHT email account Access to selected CHT Slack channels Public profile on the CHT Fellows webpage Opportunities to publish work reaching global audiences Who Should Apply? The fellowship welcomes professionals from interdisciplinary backgrounds, including but not limited to: Researchers Academics Junior faculty members Policy analysts Former government officials Journalists Writers Civil society advocates Community organizers Technology professionals AI governance experts Digital rights specialists Platform accountability experts Professionals studying technology s social impacts Relevant academic backgrounds may include: Psychology Sociology Philosophy Cognitive Science Communications Science and Technology Studies Ideal Candidate Profile Successful applicants should demonstrate: Research Excellence Applicants should have: Strong analytical skills Proven research experience Ability to deliver high-quality work on schedule Experience producing public-facing research Communication Skills Strong candidates should be able to: Explain complex ideas clearly Write for broad audiences Speak publicly Engage through social media or other communication platforms An existing audience is helpful but not required . Perspective on AI Applicants should understand: AI s societal impacts Technology governance Digital rights Policy development Human-centered AI The influence of business incentives on technology They should also demonstrate: Intellectual honesty Critical thinking Nuanced analysis Commitment to practical impact Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following: A 1–2 page research proposal for each selected topic (up to two topics). A CV or résumé (maximum two pages). A two-minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you are a strong candidate. A writing sample demonstrating research and analytical ability. Why You Should Apply This fellowship provides an exceptional platform for professionals seeking to influence one of today s most important global conversations. Participants will gain: Financial support National and international visibility Professional mentorship Media exposure Editorial support Opportunities to publish influential work Networking with interdisciplinary experts Experience contributing to responsible AI policy and public discourse Whether your expertise lies in research, journalism, policymaking, technology, or advocacy, the Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to shape how artificial intelligence develops in ways that benefit humanity. Important Dates Activity Date Applications Close July 12, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Fellowship Begins September 14, 2026 Fellowship Ends March 18, 2027 How to Apply Interested applicants should submit all required application materials before the deadline through the official application portal. Click here for more and to apply For additional information about the fellowship and the Center for Humane Technology, visit the official fellowship page. Click here for more global Fellowship opportunities The post Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
